title: Airflow, Dagster, and Prefect models
description: How a task-centric scheduler, an asset/software-defined model, and a flexible Pythonic flow model differ in abstraction.
body: Use when choosing or reasoning about an orchestrator. Airflow centers on tasks and operators with a scheduler driving DAG runs per interval; Dagster centers on software-defined assets and their dependencies, modeling data products and lineage directly; Prefect emphasizes dynamic, Pythonic flows with lighter ceremony. Do not assume DAG concepts map identically — asset-centric and task-centric models differ in how dependencies and backfills are expressed. Actions: match the model to whether you think in tasks or in data assets. Validation signals include a pipeline whose dependency model matches the tool's abstraction and reproducible backfills. Risk boundary: forcing one tool's idioms onto another breeds fragile pipelines; if the model fights the workload, escalate the choice before scaling on it.
